reason: Race-condition duplicate. An earlier scheduled-analyst run completed EXP-264 at 2026-04-27T02:09:13Z and updated the FUSE expansion-tracker.json at 02:10:20Z (output_file = EXP-264-overview-evaluate-preface-mechanical.json). This run started later and pulled origin/main at PRELUDE time, but the mechanical run's outputs had not yet been pushed to git by workspace-sync (last sync at 02:05Z; next ~06:05Z). My clean clone therefore did not see the mechanical work and produced an independent duplicate at 03:06Z (EXP-264-overview-evaluate-preface-rework.json). The two outputs are nearly byte-identical: same EP1 anchor, same EP2 anchor, byte-identical EP2 CSS, byte-identical EP1 except for one line (caption verbiage). Mechanical version uses 'Read the long form ->' (curmudgeon's recommended option for minor #5); my version uses 'Full preface ->' (deferred minor #5). Mechanical is strictly equal-or-better; this duplicate is being marked SUPERSEDED so the decider applies the canonical mechanical file pointed to by the FUSE tracker, not this duplicate. THE FUSE TRACKER IS UNCHANGED — it correctly points to the mechanical file. This proposal-file is being neutralized to prevent any chance of double-apply.
decider_action: DO NOT apply. The canonical EXP-264 is the mechanical-named file already referenced by the tracker. Workspace-sync will push both EXP-264 files (mechanical + rework) to git as new files in the append-only expansions/ directory; only the mechanical file is referenced by the tracker. The rework file is an orphaned duplicate. Optionally, the operator/integrity agent can leave the orphaned duplicate as historical evidence of the race condition (pure storage cost) or write a follow-up cleanup proposal.
curmudgeon_action: No action needed. Spot-check the canonical mechanical file per the spot-check questions still listed below (for traceability).
curmudgeon_spot_check_questions_still_ap: Does grep -c '.ds-verdict-bars .ds-vb-caption{font-size:.9rem;color:var(--text);opacity:.75;margin:0 0 .8rem}' build-scripts/generate-html.js return 1 BEFORE the patch?
Does grep -c '<section role="region" class="accuracy-math-section" aria-labelledby="accuracy-math-heading">' return 1 BEFORE the patch?
Are all 9 new classes ds-prefixed in BOTH the HTML (EP1) and CSS (EP2) segments?
After applying both patches in memory, does grep -c 'class="ds-evaluate-preface"' return 1 and grep -c 'class="evaluate-preface"' return 0?
Are the three text-color declarations on .ds-ep-heading / .ds-ep-num / .ds-ep-where switched from var(--ink-3) to var(--ink-2)?
Is the .ds-evaluate-preface left-rail (border-left:3px solid var(--ink-3)) UNCHANGED?
Is Q2 rendered with an ASCII apostrophe?
Does the canonical mechanical version's caption read 'Read the long form ->' (the curmudgeon-recommended option for minor #5)?
prior_proposal_summary_preserved_for_aud: The original (now-superseded) proposal recommended action queue-for-curmudgeon-spot-check-then-integrate, with summary 'EXP-264 rework READY. All three mechanical curmudgeon c1 holes addressed.' That recommendation still holds for the canonical mechanical file. The only substantive difference between my deferred-version and the canonical is caption verbiage; the canonical's choice is the curmudgeon-recommended one.
remediation_status: FUSE tracker UNCHANGED (no remediation needed there). FUSE status.json will be updated to note the duplicate. Clean clone will be deleted.
